steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, 18(x - 12y)(x + 12y).

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Recognize the difference of squares inside the parentheses
steps.content: The expression inside the parentheses is $x^2 - 144y^2$. Notice that $x^2 = (x)^2$ and $144y^2 = (12y)^2$, so this fits the pattern $a^2 - b^2 = (a-b)(a+b)$ with $a = x$ and $b = 12y$.

steps.title: Factor the difference of squares
steps.content: $$x^2 - 144y^2 = (x - 12y)(x + 12y).$$ Multiplying back in the outer factor of 18: $$18(x^2 - 144y^2) = 18(x - 12y)(x + 12y).$$

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: 18(x - 12y)(x - 12y) — has a middle term when expanded, so it is not the original.
  • Choice B: 18(x - 12y)(x + 12y) — is exactly the difference-of-squares form.
  • Choice C: 18(x - 3y)(x - 48y) — creates a middle term and the wrong sign on the $y^2$ term.
  • Choice D: 18(x - 3y)(x + 48y) — also creates a middle term.
That leaves Choice B — 18(x - 12y)(x + 12y).
